direction_split.note: Bull = ORB family proper (call on ORH retest). Bear-control = same entry bars faded with a put @ ORL stop — a like-for-like asymmetry control, NOT a tradeable bear signal. Confirms C3 bull-tilt if bull > bear.

honest_verdict: 3 config(s) clear ALL OP-20 bars (OOS/trade>0, posQ>=4/6, top5<200%, full-sample N>=20). Best: OTM2/8pct+exit(tp1=0.5,run=2.0,trail=0.2) (OOS/trade=$13.29, posQ=5/6, top5=103.0%, N=57; robustness 6/12 exit combos clear). CAVEAT: OOS(2026) bucket is only ~17 trades (BELOW the N>=20 bar applied to the full sample) — OOS per-trade is a thin estimate. Every clearing config is EXIT-TUNED (best of 12 exit combos per cell) — multiple-comparisons survivorship; treat as a lead to re-test OOS-forward, not a ratifiable edge. Bull-tilt CONFIRMED (C3): calls $-1.2/trade (61.7% WR) vs bear-control $-27.3/trade (23.3% WR). No DEFAULT-v15-exit cell clears; the 8% premium stop (not chart-stop-only) is what makes ORB-retest viable — consistent with the baseline thesis that the retest entry is NOT a first-strike (the adverse pullback already happened during WAITING_RETEST).

OP20_DISCLOSURE.per_trade: expectancy (per_trade avg) reported for every cell, not WR alone (OP-14)
OP20_DISCLOSURE.is_oos: IS=2025 / OOS=2026 split per cell; gate keys off OOS per-trade
OP20_DISCLOSURE.positive_quarters: out of 6 quarters in window; gate requires >=4/6
OP20_DISCLOSURE.top5_day_pct: top-5 winning DAYS as % of total P&L; gate requires <200%
OP20_DISCLOSURE.no_survivor: cells failing tiny-N / concentration / OOS-sign are marked clears_bar=false with fail_reasons (anti-pattern 2.10)
OP20_DISCLOSURE.spy_vs_option: SPY-direction != option edge; this is the option-edge test (C3/L58)
OP20_DISCLOSURE.account_scaling: qty=3 calls ~ $60-300 capital/trade; fits the $2K Safe per-trade cap
